hooks.notes: Immediate physical escape aftermath converts into formal social/legal survival: Rowan must survive the board, public blame, and procedural review.
Benn’s sister frames the issue of who gets counted and searched; Rowan recalls the deeper bell while answering about Benn, keeping the deeper mystery emotionally tied to the missing men.
Hadrik’s targeted questions confirm Pike/guild suspect maintained rails, service markers, relic fixtures, and guild-retained infrastructure in the hidden corridor.

hooks.type: Debt / Pike Leverage over Vale Household
hooks.notes: Filing fee is absurd against household debt; Rowan fears the next collector knock and now returns home to face Elias under that pressure.
“Retained notice” is now explicitly something the hall can review favorably or unfavorably, tying Rowan’s system recognition to institutional handling.
Lin Vey has not appeared yet; no change.
Lio expands the process: three-part screen, costly filing fee, sponsor/bond note signer categories, probation loss risk, and possible witness-based waiver/reduction path.
Rowan chooses to tell Benn’s family himself; Benn’s sister and Mistress Corl confront him, and Benn’s sister vows to attend the board and oppose quiet burial of the case.
Formal board scheduled for second bell; witness credibility, route ownership, Rowan’s retained notice, and search obligations will be contested there.
Elias appears at the lane, visibly frightened and angry, and orders Rowan inside just before the board.
